title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Language Lens: Resource Ledger
summary: Callbacks mutate shared maps, packages import concrete clients and global loggers, byte slices outlive response bodies, and shutdown cannot account for goroutines. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go resource ledger increment in directed owned Go crawler graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…

Competency probe: Assign owners and close order for bodies, files, timers, transports, goroutines, channels, Colly collectors, and browser-service clients.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Resource Ledger model · Theory Resource Ledger, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: defer automatically closes every resource at the correct lifetime.
